title: gypsophila perfoliata subsp. tomentosa
intro: Gypsophila perfoliata subsp. tomentosa, commonly known as woolly gypsophila, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of gypsophila perfoliata subsp. tomentosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help protect against infections. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to fully understand its effectiveness and potential side effects.
